**Claimed (appear in at least one PR's `closes_linear` field):** ENG-136, ENG-103, ENG-151, ENG-117, **ENG-134**, ENG-135, ENG-124, ENG-147, ENG-102

**Intersection (done issues that *are* claimed by at least one PR):** Only **ENG-134**

Checking ENG-134: the only PR that claims it is **#1024** (acme/web, "Rate limiter false positives: add test for rate-limiter"), and that PR **is merged**. So ENG-134 is properly closed.

**Count: 0** — No done Linear issue that is claimed by any PR is *missing* a merged PR closing it.

✅ All clear. Every done issue that any pull-request claims to close actually has a merged PR doing so.

text: Now let me compute the answer in the REPL.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Get the set of done Linear issue ids\n(def done-ids (set (map :id done-issues)))\ndone-ids"}

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Get the set of issue ids that merged PRs claim to close (non-null closes_linear)\n(def pr-closes (set (map :closes_linear (filter #(not (nil? (:closes_linear %))) merged-prs))))\npr-closes"}

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Find done issues that appear in at least one PR's closes_linear\n(def done-claimed (clojure.set/intersection done-ids pr-closes))\ndone-claimed"}

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Find done issues that are claimed by any PR's closes_linear\n(def done-claimed (filter #(contains? pr-closes %) done-ids))\ndone-claimed"}

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; For ENG-134 (the only done + claimed), check if the claiming PR is actually merged\n(def eng134-pr (filter #(= (:closes_linear %) \"ENG-134\") merged-prs))\neng134-pr"}
